meta.name: PANAS Positive and Negative Affect Schedule
meta.description: The PANAS is a 20-item scale measuring Positive Affect (PA) and Negative Affect (NA).
meta.author: Watson, Clark & Tellegen (1988)
meta.reference: Watson D, Clark LA, Tellegen A. Development and validation of brief measures of positive and negative affect. J Pers Soc Psychol. 1988;54(6):1063-1070.
meta.instruction: This scale consists of words describing feelings and emotions. Indicate to what extent you felt this way during the past week.

commonChoices.label: Very slightly or not at all

ranges.label: Low Overall Affect
ranges.description: Both PA and NA are relatively low. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Review subscale scores
Consider activities boosting positive emotions
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust

ranges.label: Moderate to High Affect
ranges.description: Overall emotional experience is moderate to high. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Review PA/NA balance
Maintain positive emotion activities
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

positive_affect.label: Low PA
positive_affect.description: Low positive affect. This indicates that your current sense of well-being and life satisfaction is relatively low. Focusing on self-care and lifestyle adjustments may be beneficial.
suggestions: Try doing one fulfilling activity each day
Stay connected with friends and family to build a sense of belonging
Explore new interests and activities
Practice a gratitude journal, focusing on positive aspects of life
If you consistently feel unmotivated, consider consulting a professional

positive_affect.label: Moderate PA
positive_affect.description: Moderate positive affect. Your well-being is at a moderate level. Increasing positive experiences and social connections may help improve your overall sense of fulfillment.
suggestions: Increase the frequency of positive social activities
Develop a hobby that gives you a sense of accomplishment
Focus on your strengths and existing resources
Set meaningful personal goals
Maintain regular exercise and outdoor activities

positive_affect.label: High PA
positive_affect.description: High positive affect. Your well-being and positive experiences are at a high level, reflecting strong psychological health and life adaptation.
suggestions: Keep up the habits and activities that bring you happiness
Share your positive experiences with others
Maintain gratitude and mindfulness practices
Focus on continued growth and deepening life meaning

negative_affect.label: Very Low NA
negative_affect.description: Very low negative affect. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

negative_affect.label: Moderate NA
negative_affect.description: Moderate negative affect. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

negative_affect.label: High NA
negative_affect.description: High negative affect. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support
